text:
WHOLE-BODY PET-CT WITH 18F-FDG
PRE-ALLOGENEIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT EVALUATION

PATIENT: Renata Kowalczyk
INDICATION: 48-year-old female with newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M4, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio. Status post 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin; day 28 bone marrow biopsy confirmed complete morphologic remission with MRD negativity by multiparameter flow cytometry. Referred for whole-body 18F-FDG PET-CT as part of pre-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT) evaluation. Clinical question: Rule out occult extramedullary AML (granulocytic/myeloid sarcoma) prior to planned myeloablative conditioning.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed at initial AML diagnosis (approximately 7 weeks prior).

TECHNIQUE: Whole-body 18F-FDG PET-CT acquired from skull base to mid-thigh using a combined PET-CT scanner. The patient fasted for a minimum of 6 hours prior to FDG administration. Serum glucose at time of injection: 104 mg/dL (within acceptable range for diagnostic quality). FDG dose administered intravenously: 10.2 mCi (377 MBq). Uptake phase: 62 minutes in a quiet, temperature-controlled environment. PET images were acquired in 3D mode with CT-based attenuation correction. CT component performed with IV contrast (100 mL iohexol 350 mg I/mL; no adverse reactions). CT technique: 120 kVp, automated mA modulation; slice thickness 3 mm (fused), 1.25 mm (diagnostic CT). PET data reconstructed using iterative reconstruction with time-of-flight and point spread function modeling. Quantitative SUV measurements performed on areas of interest using lean body mass correction (SUVbw and SUVlbm reported).

---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
The brain demonstrates the expected pattern of diffuse cortical and basal ganglia FDG uptake, physiologically symmetric. No focal intracranial areas of abnormal hypermetabolism. No leptomeningeal or dural-based FDG-avid lesions. Parotid and submandibular salivary glands show symmetric physiologic uptake. Oral mucosa demonstrates mildly increased diffuse activity, consistent with post-chemotherapy mucositis changes (clinical history of grade 3 mucositis approximately 4 weeks prior). Cervical lymph nodes: Scattered submandibular and upper cervical nodes are at the lower limits of normal in size (largest 7 mm short axis) with no discernible FDG activity above background. Neck soft tissues otherwise unremarkable. Thyroid gland: No focal uptake; no nodules or masses identified on CT.

THORAX:
Mediastinum: No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. Prevascular, paratracheal, subcarinal, and paraesophageal nodal stations are unremarkable. No discrete FDG-avid mediastinal mass or nodal aggregates. No anterior mediastinal lesion to suggest granulocytic sarcoma at this site. Trachea and central airways patent. Esophagus: No mural thickening or abnormal FDG activity.

Hila: Hilar soft tissues are symmetric and within normal limits bilaterally. No hilar adenopathy. No abnormal hilar FDG uptake.

Lungs: Lung parenchyma appears clear bilaterally. No discrete nodules, consolidative opacities, or ground-glass change. No pleural effusions. No pleural or pericardial FDG-avid lesions. No evidence of fungal or other opportunistic pulmonary infection by CT morphology. Lung windows reviewed carefully for nodules -- none identified down to 4 mm. The previously documented clear lung fields at initial staging CT are maintained.

Axillae: No enlarged or FDG-avid axillary lymph nodes bilaterally. Scattered small axillary nodes (up to 9 mm) without increased metabolic activity are noted, consistent with reactive or normal nodes.

Heart and Pericardium: Normal cardiac chamber sizes. No pericardial effusion. Physiologic FDG activity in myocardium (variable, patchy; not interpretable as pathologic). No FDG-avid pericardial or epicardial lesion.

Breast tissue: Unremarkable bilaterally. No focal mass or abnormal uptake.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is normal in size (craniocaudal span approximately 14.5 cm in midclavicular line). Parenchymal density is homogeneous on CT without focal hypo- or hyperdense lesions. FDG uptake in the hepatic parenchyma is physiologically homogeneous (background liver SUVmean approximately 2.4). No focal hepatic lesion demonstrating abnormal FDG avidity. No biliary ductal dilation. Portal vein and hepatic veins patent.

Spleen: There has been marked interval improvement in splenic size compared to the prior staging CT approximately 7 weeks ago. At that time, the spleen measured 14.2 cm and was noted to be mildly enlarged. The spleen now measures 11.8 cm in craniocaudal dimension, within the upper limits of normal, representing near-complete resolution of the prior splenomegaly. Splenic parenchyma appears homogeneous on both CT and PET. FDG uptake in the spleen is physiologically diffuse and low-level (SUVmax 2.1), without any discrete focal lesion or nodular area of increased metabolic activity. The reduction in splenic size is consistent with effective cytoreduction of the leukemic disease burden following induction chemotherapy.

Pancreas: Normal morphology. No ductal dilation. No pancreatic lesion. Physiologic FDG uptake.

Kidneys and adrenal glands: Bilateral kidneys are symmetric in size and demonstrate physiologic FDG activity with expected collecting system activity. No renal masses, hydronephrosis, or perinephric abnormality. Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in morphology; no adrenal lesion or FDG-avid focus.

Retroperitoneal and mesenteric lymph nodes: Multiple small lymph nodes are scattered throughout the retroperitoneum and mesentery, none exceeding 8 mm in short-axis diameter. None of these nodes demonstrates FDG uptake above the mediastinal blood pool background. No retroperitoneal or para-aortic adenopathy. No mesenteric nodal masses or clusters. The aorta and inferior vena cava are normal in caliber.

Bowel: No bowel wall thickening. No FDG-avid bowel lesion to suggest leukemic infiltration. Physiologic FDG activity noted in scattered bowel segments (expected). No obstruction.

PELVIS:
Inguinal lymph nodes: Bilateral inguinal nodes are within normal size limits (up to 11 mm, likely fatty hilum preserved). No pathologically elevated FDG uptake. No inguinal adenopathy.

Uterus and adnexa: The uterus is normal in size. A small intramural uterine fibroid is noted, measuring approximately 2.0 cm, without FDG avidity -- this is a benign incidental finding. No adnexal mass or ovarian lesion.

Bladder: Physiologic FDG activity in the bladder. No bladder wall abnormality.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES AND BONE MARROW:
Diffuse bone marrow FDG activity is present, as is expected in a post-chemotherapy patient approximately 4-5 weeks from the nadir of induction. There is mildly heightened marrow FDG uptake bilaterally in the posterior iliac crests (SUVmax 4.8 bilaterally, symmetric), the proximal femora, and the lower thoracic/lumbar vertebral bodies. This pattern of moderately elevated and symmetric axial and proximal appendicular marrow uptake is characteristic of active hematopoietic regeneration following intensive myelosuppressive chemotherapy and is not pathologically specific. The distribution is diffuse, symmetric, and without discrete nodular or focal areas of excessively disproportionate hypermetabolism that would raise concern for residual or relapsed leukemic disease or granulocytic sarcoma. No lytic or sclerotic bony lesions are identified on the CT component. Cortical integrity is maintained throughout the imaged skeleton. No periosteal reaction. No soft tissue extension from any osseous site.

SOFT TISSUES:
No subcutaneous or deep soft tissue masses are identified. No abnormal FDG-avid soft tissue lesion at any site that would raise concern for an extramedullary myeloid tumor (granulocytic sarcoma). The PICC line entry site in the right antecubital fossa demonstrates no surrounding abnormal FDG activity or soft tissue mass.

SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUES:
No FDG-avid skin lesions. No subcutaneous nodularity.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No FDG-avid foci identified to suggest active extramedullary AML, granulocytic (myeloid) sarcoma, or residual leukemic infiltration at any site surveyed. The study is negative for metabolically active disease.

2. Interval resolution of splenomegaly previously documented on the staging CT obtained at diagnosis approximately 7 weeks ago. The spleen has returned to normal dimensions (now 11.8 cm), consistent with an effective leukemic cytoreductive response to induction chemotherapy.

3. Diffusely increased bone marrow FDG activity, most conspicuous in the posterior iliac crests, proximal femora, and axial skeleton. This symmetric pattern of heightened marrow metabolic activity is consistent with active hematopoietic reconstitution and marrow regeneration in the post-induction setting, and is an expected physiologic finding at this interval following myeloablative chemotherapy. There is no discrete or focal nodular marrow hypermetabolism to suggest focal leukemic re-infiltration.

4. No mediastinal, hilar, axillary, retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or inguinal adenopathy.

5. No hepatic, splenic, renal, adrenal, or other visceral lesions.

6. Lungs clear. No pulmonary consolidation, nodules, or pleural effusions.

7. Incidental small intramural uterine fibroid (approximately 2.0 cm), without FDG avidity; clinically insignificant.

Overall, the metabolic imaging findings are consistent with the patient's documented complete morphologic and molecular remission following induction chemotherapy. No evidence of extramedullary leukemic disease is identified to preclude proceeding with planned allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
